classification.attack_type: Privilege Escalation via Hardcoded Role Mapper Injection
classification.attack_class: Class 10 — Improper Access Control in Admin REST API (sub-variant: mapper injection)
classification.affected_component: Admin REST API / ProtocolMappersResource + TokenManager (HardcodedRole mapper pipeline)

signals.confirmation_signal: POST /admin/realms/triage-realm/clients/{uuid}/protocol-mappers/models returned 201 Created (expected 403); token for triage-user contained realm-admin in realm_access.roles

retrieval_summary.summary: CVE-2026-4629: A user with manage-clients (but not realm-admin) can inject an oidc-hardcoded-role-mapper onto any OIDC client via the Admin REST API. The mapper causes Keycloak's TokenManager to embed realm-admin in all issued tokens for that client, granting full realm-administrator privileges to any authenticating user. Confirmed on Keycloak 26.1 — mapper injection returned 201 and issued JWT contained realm-admin.
